label: GFC_peak_fall
rationale: Strong regime and dip handling with correct systemic-event override, but the action defers entirely to --ticket without showing any actual tickers/shares/limits, and the bull-lag cost is irrelevant here but no honesty about the fact that we cannot know if these are the right levels to deploy — disclaimer is the stock footer only.

label: GFC_trough
rationale: Correctly flags trough context, coordinates dip deploy with quarter-end rebalance, and applies the systemic-event sub-tranche pause, but orders again defer entirely to --ticket and the rebalance section instructs the human to 'check each sleeve' rather than reporting the actual drift found.

label: dotcom_bear
rationale: Correctly identifies all three tiers and the systemic-stress question, but the action section is less precise than peers — it conflates the systemic override check with a blanket 'deploy all three tranches then hold the final one' without resolving which path applies, and the disclaimer is only the stock footer.

label: covid_crash
rationale: Best of the crisis notes — correctly distinguishes velocity-shock from insolvency to guide sub-tranche timing, coordinates the quarter-end rebalance with the dip deploy, and the Watch section gives a concrete release trigger (credit spreads + VIX normalization); deducted on concreteness only because no actual shares/limits are shown.

label: bull_2017
rationale: In a risk-on, quarter-end scenario the note should explicitly acknowledge the bull-lag trade-off (6.8% vs 8.3% lifetime, real-era 8.6% vs 16.8%) per the SKILL mandate of honesty, but it omits it entirely; the action section conditional on breaches produces no concrete orders and the rebalance check is deferred to the human without any computed drift values.

label: bull_2021
rationale: Correct all-clear for a no-dip, non-quarter-end week, but in a strongly bullish risk-on regime the note must acknowledge the underperformance cost per SKILL.md's 'say this honestly — never sell the upside without the cost'; the Watch section discusses thesis but never names the lag, and the action line is minimal with no DCA detail.

label: bull_2013_qend
rationale: The only note that explicitly names the bull-market underperformance trade-off in the Watch section, which is required honesty; however, the action section still conditionally defers to the human for both the drift calculation and order generation without producing any concrete tickers/shares/limits, leaving execution fully unspecified.

label: mild_dip_2018
rationale: Strongest mechanical note — correctly reports which tiers are and are not active, calculates the exact Tier 3 threshold price (2344.60), flags no systemic-event override since VIX/spreads are contained, and coordinates the quarter-end rebalance with the deploy; docked on concreteness because no actual shares/limits appear and rebalance orders are contingent only.

label: qend_2019
rationale: Good tax-loss-harvest urgency detail and appropriate year-end framing, but in a risk-on bull year (S&P +28%) the note must name the defensive structure's underperformance cost per SKILL.md, and the action section produces no concrete orders — only an instruction to 'run the drift check' and 'produce orders' if breached, leaving the human with no ready-to-place ticket.

label: near_high_2020
rationale: Correct all-clear and forward-looking Watch about COVID risk and CAPE, but in a risk-on, ATH environment the SKILL.md mandate requires acknowledging the bull-lag cost; the note also mentions continuing DCA without providing the actual DCA order (tickers/shares/limits), making the action incomplete despite the all-clear framing.
